frameworks.name: spike-timing-dependent plasticity
frameworks.one_line: STDP rules applied to inhibitory synapses from distinct GABAergic subtypes

frameworks.name: perceptual decision-making network model
frameworks.one_line: network simulation links inhibitory plasticity to decision accuracy

frameworks.name: cell-type-specific inhibitory circuit
frameworks.one_line: four GABAergic interneuron subtypes targeting L2/3 pyramidal cells

core_question: Does spike-timing-dependent plasticity differ across inhibitory interneuron subtypes, and what role does such differential plasticity play in sensory decision-making?
key_assumptions: inhibitory synapses are plastic and modifiable by activity
interneuron subtypes form functionally distinct input classes
L2/3 pyramidal cells in auditory cortex are a representative target
manipulations: presynaptic interneuron subtype (four major GABAergic classes)
input noise level in the decision-making network simulation
key_findings: likelihood of inhibitory potentiation varied across interneuron subtypes
SST+ interneuron inputs showed the most potentiation on average
simulating SST+-like plasticity preserved decision accuracy under higher input noise
interpretations: differential plasticity across inhibitory inputs supports network function
input-specific inhibitory plasticity contributes to robust sensory perception
